---
title: "T4: Exercise: Analysis of rare variants in families"
output: html_notebook
author: "Diana Barrientos González"
date: "31-05-2026"
---

We take a look at the given Pedigree.

![](/home/dbarrientos/Desktop/genomica/genomica_humana/familia3.png)

The patient has the following phenotype:

- Gross motor delay

- Global central hypotonia

- Mild dysmorphic facies

- Congenital transient torticollis

The phenotype has the typical characteristics of an autosomal recessive disorder.Therefore, we expect our individual to be homozygous, nevertheless we have to make other assumptions just in case we don't overlook something.

Since we already have the annotation of the variants specific to our patient, we need to filter the variants.

**Note:**

Genome annotations used GRCh37 (Genome Reference Consortium Human Build 37), also known as hg19. This means that genome locations are not precisely as the ones on GRCh38 also known as hg38

## DATA IMPORT

```{r}
library(readxl)
FAM3 <- read_excel("~/Desktop/genomica/genomica_humana/FAM3.xlsx")
nrow(FAM3)
```

**Total number of variants: 22548**

## VARIANT FILTERING BY ANNOTATIONS

- Remove synonymous variants

```{r}
FAM3 <- as.data.frame(FAM3)
# quitamos las variantes sinónimas como primer filtrado
filtro1 <-FAM3[FAM3[,18] != "synonymous SNV", ]
cat("El número de variantes restantes después de quitar las variantes sinónimas son:\n")
nrow(filtro1)
cat("Froma de verificar que nuestro filtrado fue bueno:\n")
table(filtro1[,18])
```

## VARIANT FILTERING BY QUALITY

The filters to consider are:

- Genotype Quality >= 30

```{r}
FAM3[,8] <- as.numeric(FAM3[,8])
FAM3[,12] <- as.numeric(FAM3[,12])
FAM3[,46] <- as.numeric(FAM3[,46])

filtro2 <-FAM3[FAM3[,18] != "synonymous SNV" & FAM3[,13] >= 30,] 
cat("The number of variants after the Q>=30 filter is:\n")
nrow(filtro2)
```

- Total Read Depth >= 10

In our dataset TR is Total Read depth

```{r}
filtro3 <-FAM3[FAM3[,18] != "synonymous SNV" & FAM3[,13] >= 30 & FAM3[,11] >= 10,] 
cat("The number of variants with a total read depth >=10 es:\n")
nrow(filtro3)
```

- The total number of TRs for the variant must match the sum of the REF and ALT alleles

```{r}
filtro4 <- filtro3[which((filtro3[,9] + filtro3[,10]) == filtro3[,11]), ]
cat("The number of variants where RR + VR == TR es:\n")
nrow(filtro4)
```

- Allele Balance > 20% ||.2 of the reads mark the ALT allele

```{r}
filtro5 <-filtro4[filtro4[,12] >= .2,] 
cat("The number of variants with ratio/Allele Balance >= 20%  es:\n")
nrow(filtro5)
```


- < 1% MAF in population studies

```{r}
filtro6 <-filtro5[filtro5[,46] < 0.01,] 
cat("The number of variants less than 1% of the MAF is:\n")
nrow(filtro6)
```


## TRIO ANALYSIS y SEGREGATION

### First assumption: our patient has an autosomal recessive disorder

Based on the type of disorder, we know that our patient is homozygous:

```{r}
filtro7 <-filtro6[filtro6[,48] == "Proband:hom",] 
cat("The number of variants after selecting a homozygous genotype in the patient is:\n")
nrow(filtro7)
```

```{r}
# heterozygous mother and father
filtro8 <-filtro7[filtro7[,49] == "Mother:het" & filtro7[,50] == "Father:het",] 
cat("The number of variants after selecting heterozygous genotypes for the parents is:\n")
nrow(filtro8)
#
```

It should be noted that there may be de novo variants that are not found in the parents, but according to this pedigree, we can exclude that the child had two de novo mutations for the same variant, allowing the option of uniparental disomy (UPD) where the person acquires two copies of a chromosome from only one parent instead of one copy from each.

Anythng that says "na" means that these variations were not seen in the mother or the father; let's rule that out.
```{r}
filtro9 <- filtro8[((filtro8[,49] != "Mother:na") | (filtro8[,50] != "Father:na")), ]
cat("Variants without two de novo mutations after parental filter na:\n")
nrow(filtro9)
```

It remains to be ruled out that the siblings are not homozygous like the proband
```{r}
#  Descartar variantes donde el probando y sus hermanos son homocigotos
filtro10<- filtro9[((filtro9[,51] != "UASib:hom") & (filtro9[,52] != "UASib:hom")), ]
cat("Variants after homozygous sibling filtering:\n")
nrow(filtro10)
```

```{r}
cat("Genes with candidate variants of autosomal recessive disorder:\n")
table(filtro10[,19])
```



### Second assumption: our patient presents with a de novo autosomal dominant disorder

If this is the case, our patient must be heterozygous
```{r}
filtro11 <- filtro6[filtro6[,48] == "Proband:het", ]
nrow(filtro11)
```
And neither parent should have the mutation

```{r}
filtro12 <- filtro11[((filtro11[,49] == "Mother:na") & (filtro11[,50] == "Father:na")),]
nrow(filtro12)
cat("There is no variant with these characteristics, we rule out that scenario")
```

### Third assumption: Compound heterozygosity: two different mutated alleles at the same genetic locus, one from each parent.

```{r}
filtro13 <- filtro6[filtro6[,48] == "Proband:het",]
nrow(filtro13)
```

Both parents cannot be homozygous
```{r}
filtro14 <- filtro13[(filtro13[,49] != "Mother:hom") & (filtro13[,50] != "Father:hom"), ]
cat("Variantes donde ningún padre es homocigoto:\n")
nrow(filtro14)

```

```{r}
filtro15 <- filtro14[duplicated(filtro14[,19]) | duplicated(filtro14[,19], fromLast = TRUE), ]
cat("Genes with variants in both parents (compound heterozygosity):\n")
nrow(filtro15)
```

```{r}
cat("Genes with candidate variants of compound heterozygosity:\n")
table(filtro15[,19])
```



### Fourth assumption: Hemizygosity: only one copy
```{r}
filtro15 <- filtro6[filtro6[,48] == "Proband:hemi", ]
cat("Homozygous variants in the proband:\n")
nrow(filtro15)
```
